title: Poincaré Duality for Closed Orientable Manifolds

solution.answer: Poincaré duality: $H^k(M;\mathbb{Z}) \cong H_{n-k}(M;\mathbb{Z})$ for closed orientable $n$-manifolds $M$.
steps: Theorem (Poincaré duality): If $M$ is a compact orientable $n$-manifold without boundary with fundamental class $[M]$, then $-\cap [M]: H^k(M;\mathbb{Z}) \to H_{n-k}(M;\mathbb{Z})$ is an isomorphism for all $k$.
Verification for $\Sigma_g$ ($n=2$): $H^0 \cong H_2 = \mathbb{Z}$, $H^1 \cong H_1 = \mathbb{Z}^{2g}$, $H^2 \cong H_0 = \mathbb{Z}$. All match. ✓
(Using UCT: $H^k(\Sigma_g;\mathbb{Z}) \cong H_k(\Sigma_g;\mathbb{Z})$ since all $H_k(\Sigma_g)$ are free.)
Implication: $\beta_k(M) = \beta_{n-k}(M)$ (Betti numbers are symmetric). For $\Sigma_g$: $(\beta_0, \beta_1, \beta_2) = (1, 2g, 1)$, symmetric around $k = 1$. ✓
For non-orientable manifolds (or manifolds with boundary), duality holds with twisted coefficients or relative cohomology.
